
title: "Midwest Data Exploration"
output:
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(pillar)
data("midwest")
```

# Data Overview

```{r}
head(midwest)
tail(midwest)
str(midwest)
names(midwest)
glimpse(midwest)
class(midwest)
```

# Bar Plots

```{r}
ggplot(midwest) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = state)) +
  labs(title = "Count of Counties by State")
```

```{r}
ggplot(midwest) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = state)) +
  labs(title = "Count of Counties by State (Horizontal)") +
  coord_flip()
```

# Histograms

```{r}
ggplot(midwest, aes(x = poptotal)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "purple", alpha = 0.7, binwidth = 5000) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Total Population")
```

```{r}
ggplot(midwest, aes(x = popdensity)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "purple", alpha = 0.7, binwidth = 10000) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Population Density") +
  theme_bw()
```

# Scatterplots

```{r}
ggplot(midwest, aes(x = area, y = popdensity)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Area vs Population Density")
```

```{r}
ggplot(midwest, aes(x = area, y = popdensity)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Area vs Population Density (Line Plot)")
```

# Stacked Bar Plot

```{r}
midwest$state <- factor(midwest$state)
midwest$category <- factor(midwest$category)

ggplot(midwest, aes(x = state, fill = category)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Categories by State") +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```
